evidence_excerpt: Restaging and subsequent CT showed recurrence and interval progression with worsening ascites and diffuse peritoneal/omental disease; developed malignant gastric outlet obstruction requiring duodenal stent and had a pulmonary embolism.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Large volume ascites (required paracentesis; abdomen mildly distended at discharge) and need for soft diet after duodenal stent indicate ongoing but manageable symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: CT scan showed interval progression with worsening ascites and diffuse peritoneal enhancement/progression of omental and peritoneal disease.
CTA chest revealed subsegmental pulmonary emboli; anticoagulation initiated (heparin, then enoxaparin).
EGD found malignant intrinsic short stenosis in the first part of the duodenum; duodenal stent was placed.
Paracentesis performed with removal of 2.5 L ascitic fluid.
After stent placement, advised soft diet to allow passage of food; paracentesis improved discomfort.
At discharge, Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.
Adjuvant gemcitabine held due to concern for pulmonary toxicity (restrictive ventilatory defect/interstitial process on PFTs).
Restaging showed recurrence of cholangiocarcinoma with peritoneal spread (omental nodularity, elevated marker).

notes_on_uncertainty: Many dates are redacted, so time periods are approximated relative to initial diagnosis. Long-term classification is based on documented progression and complications; no post-discharge follow-up details available. Functional status and symptom burden are inferred from discharge status and persistent disease findings.
